message:
feat(runtime_compat): Cx-first _with_cx variants on Mutex/RwLock (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Adds lock_with_cx, read_with_cx, write_with_cx on the runtime_compat
Mutex/RwLock primitives behind the asupersync-runtime feature. Elevates
the ambient-Cx pattern (cx::for_request() grabbed inside lock()) to an
explicit caller-provided Cx, matching the Cx-first primitives already
in place: timeout_with_cx (e4d6d62a), sleep_with_cx, Semaphore::
acquire_with_cx (183:417), mpsc::recv_with_cx.

Scope:

1. crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs (+76 source, +96 tests)

   a. `Mutex::lock_with_cx(&self, cx: &Cx) -> MutexGuard<'_, T>`

      New Cx-first entry point. Takes an explicit &Cx instead of
      deriving one via cx::for_request(). Panics with the same
      "runtime_compat mutex lock failed" message as lock() so the
      infallible-contract callers are not affected.

      Legacy `lock()` delegates to lock_with_cx() with a for_request
      Cx so the ambient-Cx callers keep working unchanged.

   b. `RwLock::read_with_cx(&self, cx: &Cx) -> RwLockReadGuard<'_, T>`

      Same pattern as lock_with_cx. Legacy `read()` delegates.

   c. `RwLock::write_with_cx(&self, cx: &Cx) -> RwLockWriteGuard<'_, T>`

      Same pattern. Legacy `write()` delegates.

2. Four new LabRuntime tests under `labruntime_sync_primitives_cx`:

   * `mutex_lock_with_cx_returns_guard_with_live_cx` (seed
     0x10C5_10C5_C410_4001): pins the happy path — guard returns,
     value observed.

   * `rwlock_read_with_cx_returns_guard_with_live_cx` (seed 4002):
     read path happy case.

   * `rwlock_write_with_cx_mutates_under_live_cx` (seed 4003):
     write_with_cx mutation visible to a subsequent read_with_cx.

   * `mutex_lock_still_works_after_cx_first_delegation` (seed 4004):
     pins the legacy lock() → lock_with_cx() delegation. Prevents a
     future refactor from accidentally diverging the two paths.

Impact: downstream modules that have a &Cx in hand can now reach the
underlying asupersync lock primitives without going through the ambient
cx::for_request() indirection. This is the foundational piece for
finishing the Cx-first migration in call sites that use these locks
(notifications mute_store, approval storage, etc.)

Verification (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):

  * cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib runtime_compat::: 188/188 pass
    (184 existing + 4 new LabRuntime)
  * cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --lib -D warnings: clean
  * rustfmt --check: clean

Leaves ft-xbnl0.2.3 still in_progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
